projects.title: Unit 9 Project — Coordinate Plane & Integers in Action
projects.desc: Apply graphing, integers, and the coordinate plane to a real-world capstone challenge for Unit 9.

projects.title: Temperature Explorer
projects.desc: Use positive and negative integers to read and compare temperatures (6.NS.5).

projects.title: Sub-Zero Ledger & Altitude Compass
projects.desc: Model integers and absolute value with altitude and below-zero values (6.NS.7).

title: Integers and Absolute Value
contentObjective: I can identify integers and find their absolute value.
languageObjective: I can explain my reasoning using the words integer, positive, negative, absolute value, and opposite.

turnAndTalk.question: Why are -5 and 5 both the same distance from zero on the number line, even though one is negative and one is positive?
turnAndTalk.kernel: -5 and 5 are the same distance from zero because ___.

stems.en: Both numbers are ___ units from zero.
stems.es: Ambos numeros estan a ___ unidades del cero.

stems.en: They are different because one is ___ and one is ___.
stems.es: Son diferentes porque uno es ___ y el otro es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student says both are 5 units from zero (same absolute value) but are opposites because of their signs.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to explain why absolute value can never be negative.
extendStems: Absolute value is never negative because ___.
The absolute value of any number tells me ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A diver is at -30 feet and a hawk is at +30 feet. How can absolute value help you describe how far each is from sea level?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Absolute value tells me how far each animal is from ___.

stems.en: The diver is ___ feet from sea level because |-30| = ___.
stems.es: El buzo esta a ___ pies del nivel del mar porque |-30| = ___.

stems.en: The hawk is ___ feet from sea level because |30| = ___.
stems.es: El halcon esta a ___ pies del nivel del mar porque |30| = ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student uses absolute value to show both are 30 feet from sea level (zero), ignoring direction.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to decide who is truly farther from sea level and justify using both the integers and their absolute values.
extendStems: They are the same distance from sea level because ___.
The sign tells me the direction, while the absolute value tells me ___.

turnAndTalk.question: How is the opposite of a number different from the absolute value of a number?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The opposite changes ___, but the absolute value gives ___.
turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student explains the opposite flips the sign while absolute value always gives the non-negative distance from zero.
turnAndTalk.extend: Push students to find a number whose opposite and absolute value are equal, and explain why.
extendStems: The opposite and absolute value are equal when the number is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Think of a real situation where a negative integer makes sense. What does zero mean in that situation?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A negative integer makes sense when ___, and zero means ___.

stems.es: En esa situacion, el cero significa ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Student gives a real context (temperature, elevation, money) and explains zero as the reference point between positive and negative.
turnAndTalk.extend: Ask students to critique whether absolute value would be useful in their chosen situation and why.
extendStems: Absolute value would help in my situation because ___.
It would tell me ___ no matter the direction.

turnAndTalk.question: During practice on Integers and Absolute Value, what strategy did you use when a problem felt tricky?
turnAndTalk.kernel: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

stems.en: The diver is ___ feet from sea level because |-30| = ___.
stems.es: El buzo esta a ___ pies del nivel del mar porque |-30| = ___.

stems.en: I knew my answer was reasonable because ___.
stems.es: Supe que mi respuesta era razonable porque ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Listen for students naming a specific strategy tied to 6.NS.7 — not just "I multiplied." They should connect steps to the key idea.
turnAndTalk.extend: How would you help a classmate who made the common mistake from today's lesson? Use one vocabulary word.
extendStems: I would tell them to ___ first because ___.
The mistake happens when you ___ instead of ___.

vocabulary.definition: A whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número entero que puede ser positivo, negativo o cero.
vocabulary.visual: ... -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3 ... shown on a number line with 0 in the center
vocabulary.cloze: A whole number or its opposite, like −2, 0, or 5, is an ___.

examples.why: It is the opposite of a whole number.
It is a fraction, not an integer.

vocabulary.definitionVi: Tập hợp các số nguyên dương, nguyên âm và số không.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مجموعة الأعداد الكاملة الموجبة والسالبة والصفر.

vocabulary.definition: A number bigger than zero, to the right of zero on a number line.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número mayor que cero, a la derecha del cero en la recta numérica.
vocabulary.visual: +5 means 5 above zero, like 5 floors above ground level
vocabulary.cloze: A number greater than zero is ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Số có giá trị lớn hơn không.

vocabulary.definition: A number smaller than zero, to the left of zero on a number line.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Un número menor que cero, a la izquierda del cero en la recta numérica.
vocabulary.visual: -3 means 3 below zero, like 3 floors underground
vocabulary.cloze: A number less than zero is ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Số có giá trị nhỏ hơn không.

vocabulary.definition: How far a number is from zero. It is never negative.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan lejos está un número de cero. Nunca es negativo.
vocabulary.cloze: The distance of a number from zero, always positive, is its ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Khoảng cách từ một số đến số 0. Nó luôn luôn là số dương.
vocabulary.definitionAr: مسافة الرقم من الصفر. وهي دائماً موجبة.

vocabulary.definition: Two numbers the same distance from zero, on opposite sides, like 3 and -3.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Dos números a la misma distancia de cero, en lados opuestos, como 3 y -3.
vocabulary.visual: 3 and -3 are opposites — both are 3 units from 0
vocabulary.cloze: Two numbers the same distance from zero but on different sides, like 4 and −4, are ___.
vocabulary.definitionVi: Hai số có cùng khoảng cách đến số 0 nhưng ngược hướng.
vocabulary.definitionAr: رقمان لهما نفس المسافة من الصفر ولكن في اتجاهين متعاكسين.

launch.narrative: Captain Vega's treasure map has clues hidden at different elevations! Some treasures are buried underground (below sea level) and some are on mountaintops (above sea level). Sea level is marked as 0. A treasure chest is at -4 (4 meters below sea level) and a lookout tower is at +6 (6 meters above sea level). How far is each from sea level?
launch.contextImage: Cross-section treasure map showing sea level at 0, buried treasure below, and a lookout tower above
noticePrompts: What does the negative sign tell you about the treasure chest's location?
Which location is farther from sea level — the chest at -4 or the tower at +6?
How can you find the distance from sea level without worrying about direction?
wonderPrompts: Can two different integers have the same absolute value?
What happens to the absolute value as numbers get farther from zero?

conceptIntro.heading: What is absolute value?
conceptIntro.intro: An integer is a whole number that can be positive, negative, or zero. The absolute value of a number is how far it is from zero on the number line. Distance is never negative.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

iDo.title: Watch me
lines: I want to find the absolute value of -6, written |-6|.
I find -6 on the number line. It sits 6 steps to the left of zero.
Distance does not care about direction, so -6 is 6 units from zero.
So |-6| = 6. Even though -6 is negative, its absolute value is positive 6.

weDo.title: Let's try together
lines: Now let's find |3|. Where is 3 on the number line? It is 3 steps to the right of zero.
How far is it from zero? It is 3 units away.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: In the explore, plot each treasure location like -4 and 6, then find its absolute value (its distance from zero).

explore.instructions: Plot each integer on the number line and find its absolute value (distance from zero).
explore.label: Plot each treasure location and find its absolute value.

discourse.prompt: How does the number line help you see the difference between an integer and its absolute value?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The integer ___ is located ___ units from zero, so its absolute value is ___. Even though ___ is negative, its distance from zero is still ___.

optional.stem: On a frosty morning at the mountain cache, the thermometer reads -8 degrees. What is the absolute value of -8?
optional.explanation: Absolute value is the distance from zero, so |-8| = 8.

optional.stem: Two treasure sites report their temperatures: -5 degrees F at the Ice Cave and -2 degrees F at the Frozen Dock. Which site is COLDER?
optional.explanation: On a number line, -5 is to the left of -2, so -5 is the smaller (colder) temperature.

optional.stem: Captain Vega compares |-12| (a deep cave) and |7| (a hilltop). Which absolute value is GREATER?
optional.explanation: |-12| = 12 and |7| = 7. Since 12 > 7, the absolute value of -12 is greater.

optional.instructions: Drag each treasure-site temperature into the right group: below freezing (colder than 0 degrees) or above freezing (0 degrees or warmer).

optional.label: Match each treasure-site temperature to its distance from zero (its absolute value).

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Colder Temperature Mistake

workedExample.label: Mission
workedExample.work: Captain Vega asks: which is colder, -8 degrees F or -2 degrees F?

workedExample.label: Crew member's reasoning
workedExample.work: 8 is bigger than 2, so -8 must be the warmer one.

workedExample.label: Crew member's answer
workedExample.work: -2 degrees F is colder than -8 degrees F.

optional.correctWork: For negative numbers, the one farther left on the number line is colder. -8 is to the LEFT of -2, so -8 degrees F is colder than -2 degrees F. Comparing the digits 8 and 2 only works for positive numbers; with negatives the order flips.
hints: Picture a number line. Which temperature sits farther to the left of zero?
With negative numbers, a larger digit (like 8) means the number is actually smaller and colder.

approaching.stem: What is the absolute value of -9?
approaching.explanation: Absolute value is the distance from zero. -9 is 9 units from zero, so |-9| = 9.

approaching.stem: Which integer has the GREATEST absolute value: -3, 7, -5, 2?

approaching.explanation: Zero is 0 units from itself on the number line, so |0| = 0.

approaching.label: Match each integer to its absolute value.
Plot these integers on the number line: -6, -2, 0, 4, 7

approaching.title: approaching — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Integers and Absolute Value.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

approaching.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

onLevel.label: Plot these integers and circle pairs that have the same absolute value: -6, 6, -2, 2, -4, 4

onLevel.instructions: Complete the table with each integer's absolute value and its opposite.

onLevel.label: Sort these integers from the GREATEST absolute value to the LEAST absolute value.

onLevel.stem: Which statement about absolute value is TRUE?
choices: |-4| = |4| because both are 4 units from zero
onLevel.explanation: Absolute value measures distance from zero. Both -4 and 4 are 4 units from zero, so |-4| = |4| = 4.

onLevel.title: onLevel — Spot the Common Mistake

workedExample.label: Read
workedExample.work: A student is working on Integers and Absolute Value.

workedExample.label: Attempt
workedExample.work: They ignored the rule: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

workedExample.label: Check
workedExample.work: The answer does not match the math rule from class.

onLevel.correctWork: Follow the key idea: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.
hints: Which step breaks the rule you learned today?
Remember: Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero.

extending.title: Find the Absolute Value Error

workedExample.label: Student thinking
workedExample.work: The number is -6, and absolute value makes it the opposite

workedExample.label: Student answer
workedExample.work: |-6| = 6, and since we take the opposite, the answer is -6

extending.correctWork: Absolute value gives the DISTANCE from zero, which is always positive or zero. |-6| = 6. The student incorrectly applied 'opposite' a second time.
hints: Absolute value measures distance. Can a distance ever be negative?
The student found 6 first, then incorrectly negated it. Distance from zero is always non-negative.

extending.prompt: A submarine is at -150 meters and a bird is flying at +150 meters. A student says the submarine is closer to sea level because -150 is less than 150. Is the student correct? Explain using absolute value.
extending.sentenceFrame: The student is ___ because absolute value measures ___. Since |-150| = ___ and |150| = ___, both are ___ from sea level. The student confused ___ with ___.

extending.label: Use absolute value to decide which side is greater, or if they are equal.
extending.instructions: Calculate each side. Left: |-8| + |3| = 8 + 3 = 11. Right: |7| + |-4| = 7 + 4 = 11. Are they balanced?

optionalActivity.name: Integer Temperature Mission
optionalActivity.intro: Captain Vega's crew logs temperatures at every treasure site, from icy caves to sun-baked beaches.

practice.commonMistake: A common mistake in Integers and Absolute Value is skipping the key idea: "Absolute value tells you the distance from zero, so it is always positive or zero." — always check your work against this rule before you submit.

connect.scenario: A submarine is at -150 meters (below sea level) and a helicopter is at +200 meters (above sea level). A rescue team needs to know how far each is from sea level to plan a mission.
connect.promptQuestion: How does absolute value help the rescue team compare the submarine and helicopter positions?
connect.prompt: The submarine is at ___ meters, and its absolute value is ___ because ___. The helicopter is at ___ meters, and its absolute value is ___. Absolute value helps the rescue team because ___.

exitTicket.stem: Which integer has an absolute value of 8 and is located to the LEFT of zero on the number line?
exitTicket.explanation: To the left of zero means negative. The integer -8 has an absolute value of 8 because it is 8 units from zero.

printables.desc: Solve each problem, then color by the answer to reveal a picture.
